name: Apple Intelligence Personal Context (iOS 26)
overview: Apple Intelligence Personal Context is Apple's privacy-first personal AI layer announced at WWDC 2024 and re-positioned at WWDC 2025 (June 9, 2025) under the Liquid Glass design language of iOS 26 / iPadOS 26 / macOS Tahoe 26. It bundles three pillars: (1) the Foundation Models framework that lets third-party apps call a ~3B parameter on-device LLM with @Generable / @Guide Swift macros and Tool Calling at zero per-request cost, (2) a Private Cloud Compute path for tasks too heavy for-device, and (3) a Siri-side 'Personal Context' semantic index over Calendar / Mail / Messages / Photos / Contacts that enables proactive, on-screen-aware, in-app actions. The Siri personalisation pillar was officially delayed to spring 2026 (WWDC 2026 timeframe), while the Foundation Models framework and Liquid Glass shipped on schedule with iOS 26 in September 2025.
originator_or_company: Apple Inc. Public faces: Craig Federighi (SVP Software Engineering, WWDC keynote owner), Tim Cook (CEO), John Giannandrea (SVP Machine Learning & AI Strategy), Robby Walker (Senior Director, Siri — internally called the delays 'ugly' and 'embarrassing'), Mike Rockwell (took over Siri after the March 2025 delay reshuffle, ex-Vision Pro lead). Foundation Models research org: Apple Machine Learning Research (machinelearning.apple.com). Liquid Glass design heritage: Alan Dye (VP Human Interface Design) team, evolving the Jony-Ive era HIG. Released as part of iOS 26 / iPadOS 26 / macOS Tahoe 26 / watchOS 26 / visionOS 26 / tvOS 26 (Sept 2025).

[WWDC 2025: Apple Says Personalized Siri Features Are Still Not Ready - MacRumors](https://www.macrumors.com/2025/06/09/personalized-siri-still-not-ready/)

[Apple Intelligence AI False Advertising Class Action - Clarkson Law Firm](https://clarksonlawfirm.com/lp/apple-intelligence-false-advertising/)
core_mechanics: (1) ON-DEVICE LLM — A ~3B parameter transformer model with KV-cache sharing and 2-bit quantization-aware training, optimized for Apple silicon (A17 Pro and later, M1 and later). 4,096 token context window. Runs offline, no per-request cost. (2) FOUNDATION MODELS FRAMEWORK (Swift) — Native Swift API. Three primitives: (a) `@Generable` macro on a struct/enum produces type-safe structured output via constrained decoding (the model is forced at the token level to produce schema-valid JSON); (b) `@Guide` annotation provides natural-language hints per property; (c) `Tool` protocol allows the model to autonomously call developer-defined Swift functions (with structurally-correct arguments — hallucinated tool names are impossible). (3) ADAPTER TRAINING — Apple ships a LoRA adapter training toolkit so developers can fine-tune the on-device model for domain-specific tasks while still distributing only the small adapter weights. (4) PRIVATE CLOUD COMPUTE (PCC) — When a query exceeds on-device capacity, only the relevant data is sent to Apple silicon servers running a verifiable PCC stack; software images are publicly auditable; no logs, no Apple staff access, data wiped after response. (5) SEMANTIC INDEX (Personal Context, delayed) — Built on Core Spotlight, indexes Calendar / Mail / Messages / Photos / Contacts / Notes / third-party App Intents donations. Powers Siri's 'fill out this form from my driver's license photo', 'when's mom's flight', and on-screen awareness behaviors. Per Mark Gurman / Bloomberg: targeted for spring 2026 (likely iOS 26.4). (6) APP INTENTS BRIDGE — Third-party apps expose actions via the App Intents framework; Siri (post-2026) and Shortcuts can chain them based on personal context. Foundation Models can already call App Intents today via Tool Calling. (7) LIQUID GLASS UI MATERIAL — A translucent, real-time-refractive material that lenses background pixels, adds specular highlights driven by device motion sensors, and adaptive shadows. Used on Tab Bar / Navigation Bar / Sidebar / Sheet / Alert / App Icon. SwiftUI APIs: `.glassEffect()`, `GlassView` (expo-glass-effect on RN). HIG explicitly forbids glass-on-glass and warns against glass on dense content. (8) PROACTIVE SUGGESTION SURFACES — Lock Screen Live Activities, Smart Stack widgets, Siri Suggestions, Spotlight, Smart Replies in Mail / Messages. Foundation Models lets third-party apps generate suggestions for these surfaces based on local data without ever sending it to a server. (9) SAFETY GUARDRAILS — System prompt 'Do not hallucinate', adversarial-data fine-tuning for refusal behavior, content classifiers, age-gated content controls. Acceptable Use Policy for Foundation Models prohibits medical advice, legal advice, financial transactions, surveillance.
designer_or_researcher_voice: Craig Federighi (WWDC 2025 keynote, June 9 2025): 'As we've shared, work needed more time to reach our high quality bar, and we look forward to sharing more about it in the coming year.' — the only on-stage acknowledgement of the Personal Context delay.

Robby Walker (Apple Senior Director of Siri, internal all-hands meeting, March 2025, reported by Bloomberg): the delay of personalized Siri features was 'ugly' and 'embarrassing' and represented a moment where 'we had not delivered.'

Apple Foundation Models tech report (arXiv 2507.13575, July 2025): 'We applied 2-bit quantization-aware training and KV-cache sharing to the ~3B parameter on-device model so it could run within the memory and thermal envelope of A17 Pro and M1-class silicon while preserving instruction-following quality.'

John Gruber (Daring Fireball, March 8 2025): 'These features were the centerpiece of the Apple Intelligence marketing campaign — they were in the keynote, in the ads, on the iPhone 16 box. To delay them is, in marketing terms, an embarrassment.'

Apple HIG, Liquid Glass guidance (developer.apple.com/design): 'Liquid Glass is a dynamic material that responds to its surroundings, providing a sense of depth and hierarchy. Use it on navigation, controls, and overlays — never on dense reading content where it would compromise legibility.'

Jakob Nielsen / Raluca Budiu (NN/g, October 2025): 'Liquid Glass is cracked. Translucency reduces text contrast on busy backgrounds, and motion-driven specular highlights become a distraction during reading-heavy tasks. We measured a 15% increase in time-on-task on Settings screens compared to iOS 18.'
metrics_or_evidence: Foundation Models adoption: at WWDC 2025 Apple announced 'tens of thousands of developers' began experimenting in beta within weeks of release; by Apple's September 2025 newsroom post, named partners include AllTrails (turn-by-turn nature suggestions), Lil Artist (children's story generator), CellWalk (biology learning), Grammo (English grammar tutor), VisionKit, Stuff (task manager). Model size & cost: ~3B parameters on-device, 4,096-token context, $0 per-request; server model is a Parallel-Track Mixture-of-Experts transformer running on Apple silicon servers. Performance: per Apple Foundation Models 2025 update post, on-device model is preferred over Phi-3-mini and Gemma-2-2B in human pairwise eval but trails Qwen-3-4B; English summarization MMLU-Pro within 4 points of much larger models thanks to instruction-following adapters. Liquid Glass impact: NN/g October 2025 study reports 15% increase in time-on-task on iOS 26 Settings vs iOS 18, citing readability regression on translucent surfaces. Personal Context delay legal exposure: U.S. class action filed March 2025 (Clarkson Law Firm; San Jose California), Apple motion to dismiss filed September 2025, court still pending as of April 2026; plaintiffs claim ~iPhone 16 buyers paid a premium for advertised features that did not ship. Japan availability: Apple Intelligence Japanese language support shipped iOS 18.4 (March 2025); iPhone 16 series saw exceptional traction in Japan per Omdia 2026 data. [uncertain: precise developer-adoption count, NN/g exact 15% figure not yet peer-reviewed]
criticisms: (1) DELIVERY GAP — The signature Personal Context / on-screen-awareness / in-app-action Siri features were headline-marketed at WWDC 2024 and on iPhone 16 packaging, then quietly delayed in March 2025 with no firm new date. WWDC 2025 confirmed 'in the coming year' (~spring 2026). Mark Gurman, John Gruber, and most major outlets called this Apple's biggest software credibility hit since MobileMe. (2) HALLUCINATION RISK — Despite explicit 'Do not hallucinate' system prompts (leaked August 2024), the on-device model still produces plausible-but-false summaries; high-profile cases include false BBC News notification summaries (BBC formally complained) and a misleading Washington Post headline summary. Apple paused notification summaries for news category in early 2025. (3) LIQUID GLASS LEGIBILITY — NN/g, Reddit r/ios, and accessibility advocates report reduced contrast and increased visual noise; Apple shipped a 'Reduce Transparency' setting as a partial fix. (4) UNEVEN AVAILABILITY — Apple Intelligence still gated behind A17 Pro+ / M1+ silicon, leaving the iPhone 15 / 15 Plus / older iPads excluded. EU and China launches lagged due to DMA / regulatory negotiation. (5) SMALL MODEL CEILING — 3B parameters cannot match GPT-4o-class reasoning; for non-trivial questions Apple punts to ChatGPT integration (opt-in). For an exam-prep product this means complex 簿記2級 仕訳問題 explanations may exceed on-device capability. (6) PRIVATE CLOUD COMPUTE OPACITY — While Apple publishes images for audit, in practice independent verifiability is limited to a small set of researchers. Matthew Green (Johns Hopkins) called PCC 'a real engineering effort' but warned it is 'still a black box for end users'.
failure_cases: (1) NEWS NOTIFICATION SUMMARIES PAUSED — Jan 2025, Apple disabled Apple Intelligence summaries for the News & Entertainment category after BBC, NYT, and Washington Post protests over fabricated headlines (e.g. 'Luigi Mangione shoots himself' — false). Re-enabled in iOS 18.3 with explicit 'AI summary' label and italics formatting. (2) DELAYED SIRI PERSONALIZATION — Originally promised iOS 18 fall 2024, delayed in March 2025 to 'coming year', then to spring 2026 per Bloomberg reporting; multiple Apple TV / HomePod hardware refreshes reportedly held back waiting for personalized Siri. (3) CLASS ACTION LAWSUIT — Clarkson Law Firm filed a putative class action in San Jose claiming false advertising on Personal Context features that shipped on iPhone 16 marketing but never reached users; Apple's motion to dismiss is still pending as of April 2026. (4) IMAGE PLAYGROUND BACKLASH — Genmoji and Image Playground produced racially insensitive and trademarked-character outputs at launch; Apple silently restricted prompts and added human filtering layers. (5) WRITING TOOLS REWRITE WRONGNESS — Users reported Writing Tools 'Make It Friendly' inserting fabricated names and invented dates into draft emails; corrected over multiple point releases. (6) LIQUID GLASS REDUCED-TRANSPARENCY OPT-OUT — Apple shipped a system-wide 'Reduce Transparency' toggle in iOS 26.0.1 within weeks of GA, effectively a soft retreat from the maximalist version of the design.
antipatterns: (1) DON'T promise Personal-Context-grade features in marketing before they ship — this is the textbook Apple-2024-Siri trap; tie demos to enable-flag-flipped real builds only. (2) DON'T lean on the on-device 3B model for high-stakes correctness (medical, legal, exam answer-grading) — use it for drafting, suggestion, ranking, and summarization where a wrong output is recoverable; route high-stakes flows to a deterministic engine or larger server model. (3) DON'T stack Liquid Glass on dense reading content (problem text, explanations, long-form). HIG explicitly reserves glass for navigation chrome. Putting glass behind 簿記の仕訳 problem text will reduce comprehension. (4) DON'T build features that REQUIRE personal context across calendars / mail / messages until the Siri V2 architecture (spring 2026) actually ships — fall back to in-app data. (5) DON'T duplicate Apple's notification-summary pattern in a learning app. Summarizing user-generated study notes is fine; summarizing third-party news / textbooks for delivery to other users is risky. (6) DON'T pretend the model is doing more than it is (no fake 'thinking' loaders, no overstated confidence in answer correctness). Apple's HIG 'Generative AI' guidelines warn against false-confidence visual cues. (7) DON'T tie monetization to opt-in personal context sharing in a way that pressures users — the EU AI Act + DSA dark-pattern rules apply.
applicability_to_shikaq: HIGH visual-and-philosophical fit, MEDIUM-HIGH mechanical fit. shikaq is iPad-first / Apple-HIG-only / privacy-friendly, exactly the persona Apple Intelligence is built for. Concrete translation playbook for the 20代女性キャリアアップ / iPad cafe / 月額980円 context: (a) FOUNDATION MODELS FOR QUIZ DRAFTING — use `@Generable` to generate ITパスポート / 基本情報 / SPI / 簿記2級 draft questions on-device from the user's own notes (Obsidian-imported markdown stored locally on iPad). The admin can still hand-publish via the LMS, but learners get personal practice variants for free, instantly, offline — which is a marketing wedge against Udemy and 分厚い参考書. (b) PROACTIVE STUDY REMINDERS via App Intents donations — donate 'review 5 vocab cards' / 'finish chapter 3 quiz' actions to Siri Suggestions / Smart Stack / Spotlight; let Apple's system surface them at the right moment based on personal context (Calendar gap before exam, walking-to-cafe routine). This achieves Duolingo-grade reminder leverage WITHOUT shikaq sending push spam. (c) ON-DEVICE EXPLAINER — when a user asks 'なぜこの仕訳になるの' on a published explanation, route to the 3B model with the lesson context as a Tool to fetch related lesson chunks; falls back to a server model only if the user has subscribed and the answer needs depth. (d) LIQUID GLASS ON CHROME — Sidebar / Tab Bar / Toolbars only; Practice / Lesson / Today reading panes stay on solid systemBackground per HIG. (e) PERSONAL CONTEXT (post-2026) — when Siri V2 ships, donate study-progress entities so the learner can ask Siri '今日のITパスポートのリマインダー何' and get a context-aware answer. (f) PRIVACY MARKETING — make 'すべて端末で処理。データは外に出ません' a top-of-fold landing-page bullet; resonates with the Japanese 20s-female persona's documented privacy preference. HEXAD coverage: Achiever (instant variant quizzes), Free Spirit (offline + privacy + no upsell), Philanthropist (share generated explainers with study group, opt-in), Player (no streak coercion needed because surfacing happens passively on system canvas). Avoid Disruptor / public competition modes.
phase_assignment: Phase 2 (4-6 weeks). Phase 1 should ship the static curriculum + Liquid Glass chrome + Activity Rings adaptation without any Apple Intelligence dependency, because (a) the framework requires iOS 26 + A17 Pro / M1 silicon, narrowing reach, and (b) Personal Context Siri features don't ship until spring 2026 (~iOS 26.4). Phase 2 introduces Foundation Models-powered features behind a feature flag: on-device draft quiz generation, lesson explainer, Smart Reply for the AI Q&A screen. Phase 3 (6-8 weeks) adds App Intents donation + Siri Personal Context integration (exact timing depends on Apple's spring 2026 release). Rationale: build the product to work fully without Apple Intelligence first, then layer it as 'magic on supported devices', following the Apple Books / Apple Notes pattern of progressive enhancement.
integration_points: Existing components: shikaq-app/components/screens/Practice.tsx (target for on-device quiz draft generation, '+5問' button), screens/AIQA.tsx (replace placeholder with Foundation Models call routed via a thin Swift Native Module, falling back to DeepSeek server-side on Android / non-Apple-Intelligence devices), screens/Audio.tsx (use on-device summarization to generate audio script outline before TTS), Sidebar.tsx (Liquid Glass already applied via .liquid-glass; verify HIG compliance). New native module: shikaq-app/modules/AppleIntelligence/ — a small Expo Native Module exposing Foundation Models requests, gated by Platform.OS === 'ios' && supportsAppleIntelligence(). DB tables: add `ai_drafts` (id, user_id, certification_id, source_lesson_id, prompt_hash, generated_payload jsonb, created_at, accepted_bool) — store on-device-generated draft quizzes for review-then-publish flow when admin edits. App Intents: define `ReviewVocabIntent`, `StartPracticeSessionIntent`, `OpenLessonIntent` for Siri donations. RLS: own-data on ai_drafts; admin can read aggregate. Routes: /practice (draft generation entrypoint), /aiqa (lesson explainer), /settings/ai (toggle Apple Intelligence on/off, surface battery / device requirements). Edge Functions: server fallback for non-Apple-Intelligence devices using DeepSeek with the same Generable schema.
risks: (1) DEVICE REACH — A17 Pro+ / M1+ excludes ~30-40% of the 20代女性 iPad install base (older Air / mini / 9th-gen). Without a server fallback, the feature reads as 'broken' on these devices. Mitigation: identical schema + DeepSeek server route; degrade gracefully. (2) SIRI V2 SLIPPAGE — Apple's track record on Personal Context shipping is poor; if it slips past spring 2026, any roadmap that depends on App Intents donations needs a fallback marketing message. Mitigation: keep Phase 3 features behind a feature flag. (3) HALLUCINATION ON EXAM CONTENT — 簿記2級仕訳 / 基本情報科目B のアルゴリズム are exactly where a 3B model fabricates plausible-but-wrong answers. Mitigation: never ship AI-generated content as 'graded' — admin must accept the draft into published status; mark draft origin clearly in admin UI. (4) LIQUID GLASS LEGIBILITY ON DENSE TEXT — putting glass behind problem text or explanations will measurably reduce comprehension (NN/g 15% time-on-task regression). Mitigation: solid backgrounds for problem / explanation panes; glass only on chrome. (5) APP STORE / FALSE-ADVERTISING ECHO — marketing 'Apple Intelligence powered' before features actually work end-to-end on day-1 invites the same Clarkson-class-action exposure Apple is fighting. Mitigation: launch with 'Foundation Models powered draft questions (iPhone 15 Pro + and iPad M1+)' rather than 'AI-powered Siri integration', and only add the Siri claim when shipped. (6) PRIVATE CLOUD COMPUTE TRUST — using PCC for any task requires audit-aware messaging; some EU users may distrust opaque 'cloud' even with Apple's audit story. Mitigation: clearly label which features are on-device, which use PCC, which use OpenAI/DeepSeek. (7) ACCEPTABLE USE POLICY — Apple bans medical / legal / financial advice for Foundation Models. 簿記2級 explanations are borderline 'financial education' but not 'financial advice'; should be safe but worth a legal check before launch. (8) PERFORMANCE — Foundation Models inference takes 1-3 seconds even on M2; UX must show streaming output or anchored skeleton state.
apple_hig_compatibility: compatible. Apple Intelligence + Foundation Models + Liquid Glass IS the iOS 26 HIG canonical pattern. SF Pro / SF Symbols / Material Symbols (web) / NativeWind 4 / expo-glass-effect / expo-blur all align. Three explicit adaptations: (a) Liquid Glass only on chrome (Tab Bar / Sidebar / Toolbar / Sheet) and never behind problem text or explanations — HIG explicitly reserves glass for navigation surfaces and warns against dense-reading-content placement. (b) Use semantic colors (label / systemBackground / systemBlue / certification tints) — never hardcode Apple Intelligence's signature gradient mark in third-party UI. (c) Honor Reduce Transparency, Reduce Motion, Increase Contrast accessibility settings — Apple's own iOS 26.0.1 walked back some glass intensity in response to these signals. The Foundation Models adoption itself has zero HIG conflict; Apple actively encourages it.
ethical_score: positive. Personal Context's design philosophy — on-device-first, Private Cloud Compute as fallback, no logs, no Apple-staff access, App Intents instead of screen scraping — is the closest a Big Tech AI initiative has come to Center for Humane Technology's stated principles (data minimization, user control, opt-in). Negative levers: (a) the 'magic suggestion' surface area is large enough that means-ends reversal (study to please the system, not yourself) is possible, (b) Liquid Glass motion can amplify visual restlessness for ADHD users, (c) Apple's marketing-vs-shipping gap on the Siri side erodes user trust in 'AI promises' generally. For shikaq specifically, the privacy story is a net-positive marketing wedge against Udemy / スタディサプリ (which both ship behavioral telemetry to vendors). Score moves to clearly positive when shikaq labels which features run on-device vs PCC vs server, ships Reduce Transparency support, and never gates monetization on AI-feature usage.
evidence_quality: company_case_study with theoretical underpinning. Apple Foundation Models tech report (arXiv 2507.13575, 2025) is a non-peer-reviewed but quantitatively detailed model card. WWDC 2025 sessions are vendor-authoritative but vendor-biased. NN/g Liquid Glass usability writeup (October 2025) is a small-N expert review, not an RCT. Class-action legal filings are evidence of consumer perception, not feature efficacy. The mechanism 'on-device LLM + structured generation can power high-quality, private app features' is supported by independent developer tutorials (Natasha the Robot, Artem Novichkov, AzamSharp, AppCoda) and live shipping apps (AllTrails, Grammo, CellWalk) — credible but anecdotal. No rigorous RCT exists comparing on-device vs server LLM education outcomes as of April 2026.
localization_fit_japan: high. Apple Intelligence Japanese-language support shipped in iOS 18.4 (March 2025); the on-device 3B model handles Japanese tokenization and instruction-following at production quality. iPhone 16 / iPad M-series adoption among the 20代女性 career-track segment in Japan is among the highest globally per Omdia 2026 data. Japanese cultural fit notes: (a) the 'すべて端末内で処理' privacy framing resonates with documented Japanese reluctance to share personal data with foreign cloud services (LINE incident 2024 cemented this), (b) Liquid Glass aesthetic aligns with Japanese affinity for translucent / 半透明 / 影 / 余白 design vocabulary (cf. Muji, Sou Sou, MUJI to GO), (c) Siri's V2 personalisation will support Japanese on launch per WWDC 2025 statements, no second-class-citizen lag like the iOS 18 launch. Risk: Japanese users are particularly intolerant of hallucinations in exam-prep contexts (試験対策で間違った答えを出されたら学習効果以前に信頼を失う) — strong guardrails on AI-generated content marked 'draft' / '校閲前' / '参考' are mandatory. Recommend: ship Foundation Models features with explicit 「AIによる下書き・正確性は校閲後に保証」 disclaimer, mirror Apple's own 'AI Summary' italic-label pattern.
